reasoning: At 8x zoom, multiple clear AI tells detected. (1) Gradient shading: gem bodies show smooth pink→light-pink→white transitions; clean pixel art should use 2-3 hard palette steps or explicit dithering — no dither pattern visible, just smooth blending. (2) AA edges: at 8x each source pixel should render as a solid 8×8 block; several gem outlines and interior tone boundaries show partial-pixel color mixing, indicating sub-pixel anti-aliasing baked into the source data. (3) Melting details: specular highlight blobs are soft ovals rather than crisp 1-2 px hard dots — characteristic AI 'blur halo' around bright spots. (4) Inconsistent outlines: some sprites have clean 1px dark outlines, others are thinner or missing on one side, and a few smaller sprites lack outlines entirely. (5) Color bleed: pink hue bleeds into the near-black shadow pixels on several gems, producing muddy border pixels instead of a clean dark tone. Semantic read is fine (recognisably a ruby/gem), background is acceptably clean, and overall silhouettes are coherent, but the shading and edge quality are below acceptable pixel-art standards for a shipped game asset.
